metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_744
metadata.comment:
Rationale: SV31 actively transports zinc (zn2+) in a proton-dependent manner.

SV31 is able to bind zinc: Recombinant SV31 expression in NGF-differentiated PC12 cells and measurement of zinc binding activity using a cell-permeable zinc indicator FluoZin-3. Comparison of wild-type SV31 and mutated SV31 (putative zinc-binding sites were mutated) revealed significantly reduced zinc-binding capacities for the mutant (Fig.6c).

SV31 is able to transport zinc in a proton-dependent manner (Fig.7): SV31 proteoliposomes acidified to pH 6.0 containing the indicator FluoZin-1 exhibit an accumulation of luminal fluorescence after addition of 100 uM Zn2+. SV31 proteoliposomes at pH 8.0, and proteoliposomes containing mutated SV31 acidified to pH 6.0 do not exhibit an accumulation of fluorescence after zinc application.. Experimental description: ???
I am guessing "rat", because in their first paper I found the following information: The open reading frame of the rat SV31 gene (database accession number XM_341114) was synthesized by Epoch Biolabs...

6/6/2017 Pim
- Email sent to Walther Volknandt for info on the species of the SV31 protein featured in the paper. The author replied:

"the species is rat
Best
Walter Volknandt"
- Species set to *rat* accordingly
